title: crotalaria burhia
intro: Crotalaria burhia, also known as Burhia weed, is a plant species in the pea family, Fabaceae, characterized by its yellow or greenish-yellow flowers and hairy leaves. It is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Crotalaria burhia include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial effects against various pathogens, while also being used to treat skin conditions and wounds. Crotalaria burhia is typically prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and can also be used in the form of tinctures or powdered extracts for medicinal purposes.
